global_constraints: Do not create a BaselineApprovalRecord until the normative package is complete and a consistency review is green.
Do not change runtime/host-orchestrator behavior during P0A planning or normative closure tasks.
Do not create runtime/local-ai-runtime before Baseline Approval and the P0B Truth Reset.
Do not claim Implementation Acceptance, Full Q0, P2 admission, live acceptance, or cutover without their durable records.
Keep each implementation slice reviewable; five primary files is a target, not a mechanical failure gate.
Preserve user changes and roll back only the current task diff.

new_runtime: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime ruff check runtime/local-ai-runtime

work_items.title: Close the baseline lineage record
work_items.goal: Archive the exact v3.17 bytes when available, independently verify them, and materialize BaselineLineage.v1 without promoting a provisional transcript hash to a content hash.
preconditions: v3.21 candidate bytes and SHA-256 still match planning-status.json
No active BaselineApprovalRecord exists

in: v3.14-v3.17 lineage, both conflicted v3.18 archives, the exact v3.19 and frozen v3.20 archives, and the v3.21 candidate identity

acceptance: v3.17 and both distinct v3.18 archives are byte-for-byte grounded or the task stops with missing_source_bytes
v3.19 is recorded as a 111952-byte superseded candidate with its verified SHA-256
v3.20 is recorded as a 130890-byte superseded candidate with SHA-256 43cb98737daa5d171a9cda2dca49c8f118fb8be92745b4076948d9178e56a130
The record distinguishes canonical_predecessor, withdrawn_candidate, superseded_candidate and withdrawn_draft
The provisional v3.17 transcript hash is never labeled content_sha256
Package inventory marks P0A-LINEAGE present only after schema and dual-hash verification
work_items.rollback: Remove only the new lineage artifact and revert its inventory/status/evidence references.
stop_conditions: Exact v3.17 source bytes cannot be recovered
Independent hashes disagree
Any source requires silent normalization
prohibited_actions: Do not reconstruct v3.17 from memory
Do not edit the v3.19 or v3.20 archives or the v3.21 candidate
Do not create an approval record

work_items.title: Define BaselineManifest and normative package closure rules
work_items.goal: Create the manifest schema, byte/version rules, fixtures and verifier skeleton without creating the final BaselineManifest instance before the package review head is frozen.
preconditions: BaselineLineage.v1 is present
v3.21 candidate hash remains unchanged

in: Narrative/artifact/package version boundaries and domain-separated hashes
out: Approval semantics beyond the record schema

acceptance: Validator rejects BOM, CR, non-NFC, trailing SP/HTAB, disallowed controls and wrong terminal LF
Duplicate artifact IDs and paths are rejected
Manifest schema requires every entry to bind artifact ID/version, byte count, SHA-256, schema version and verifier ID
The manifest does not embed its own hash in its hashed payload
Manifest schema requires a frozen package_review_head and forbids review/approval self-reference
No final BaselineManifest.v1.json exists and P0A-MANIFEST remains missing
The standalone verifier skeleton validates bytes/schema fixtures and P0A-VERIFIER remains in_progress, not present
work_items.rollback: Revert only manifest schema/fixtures/verifier-skeleton and inventory references.
stop_conditions: Canonical byte rules contradict v3.21
A manifest cycle cannot be removed without changing the baseline
prohibited_actions: Do not normalize source bytes in place
Do not create the final BaselineManifest instance
Do not mark P0A-MANIFEST present or the package complete

work_items.title: Close canonical JSON, Git path and Windows identity contracts
work_items.goal: Materialize CanonicalizationPolicy.v1 and its positive/negative fixtures, including duplicate-key rejection, ordered-array semantics, declared set semantics and independent NTFS collision keys.
preconditions: Manifest conventions are fixed

in: Windows path and repo identity canonicalization

acceptance: Arrays preserve order unless schema declares set_semantics
Duplicate set keys reject instead of silently deduplicating
Git path spelling is validated but never normalized
NTFS collision key is separate from Git path
Alias-aware 8.3 verification uses handle identity and rejects collisions, alias bypass and post-qualification drift
An unreadable volume-level 8.3 policy records policy_query_denied and falls back to non-elevated alias/identity probes
The contract does not require globally disabling short-name creation
Specification and canonical JSON strings reject unapproved Cc/Cf, bidi, zero-width, BOM and Unicode noncharacters
limit-1, limit and limit+1 fixtures exist for all bounded fields
work_items.rollback: Revert only the canonicalization bundle and manifest entry.
stop_conditions: Windows case mapping algorithm is not pinned
Fixture behavior depends on host locale
prohibited_actions: Do not case-fold Git paths
Do not silently deduplicate JSON sets

work_items.title: Close product, routing, template, submission and resubmission contracts
work_items.goal: Define Unified Native/Batch routing, closed template parameters, permanent submission identity and the atomic TaskResubmission relation.
preconditions: CanonicalizationPolicy.v1 is fixed
acceptance: Ordinary duplicate submit permanently returns root_task_id
TaskResubmission uniqueness is scoped to the resubmission relation
Replay checks an existing successor before current-task guards

Submission admission is bounded parse, canonicalize public envelope, volatile existing-family lookup, authorized replay, and only for an absent family current membership, schema, secret scan, qualification and transactional recheck/create
Existing-family replay remains stable after template catalog, secret scanner, qualification or Authorization generations change
Rejected submissions create no family and retain no parameters, ordinary digest or stable oracle
Parameters cannot control executable, argv, env, permissions, gates, refs, model, provider or sandbox
Historical task-ref success permanently blocks resubmission
work_items.rollback: Revert only the product/submission bundle and manifest references.
stop_conditions: Any parameter field can influence the execution surface
Response-loss replay can create a second successor
prohibited_actions: Do not add free-text parameters
Do not add a two-stage permit without a new approved requirement

work_items.title: Close qualification, environment and Authorization contracts
work_items.goal: Define reproducible qualification input closure, immutable environment bindings, keyring-only auth and reusable Authorization with continuation rules.
preconditions: Submission identity is fixed

in: Codex sandbox opaque-state and diagnostic contract

acceptance: Present, absent, expanded and blocked input entries are exhaustive
QualificationObservation binds the observed base commit/tree and alias evidence while the reusable QualificationSensitiveInputSet excludes base, task and submission identity
A fresh observation with byte-identical sensitive/environment/instruction/policy hashes refreshes last_observed_base by CAS without incrementing qualification or Authorization generation
The reusable qualification set binds resolver catalog and negative discovery and changes generation only when a safety-relevant bound hash changes
Dirty/staged/untracked sensitive inputs block qualification
CodexSandboxStateBinding separates immutable config, managed sandbox state and broker-only secrets without exporting secret metadata
sandbox.log is an opaque bounded diagnostic: task processes cannot read it and ordinary evidence stores neither content nor content hash
Authorization revoke and effect grants share one lock/transaction linearization order
Normal root grants use basis_kind=active_authorization and bind one exact effect identity
A committed grant can only complete, terminate or reconcile its exact bound effect
Authorization checks exist at every irreversible stage
Continuation cannot authorize another writer
work_items.rollback: Revert only qualification/auth artifacts and manifest references.
stop_conditions: Input discovery closure is ambiguous
Authorization fingerprint cannot be independently recomputed
prohibited_actions: Do not hash dirty working-tree content into Authorization
Do not persist credentials

work_items.title: Close process, Job, marker, fencing and adoption contracts
work_items.goal: Define atomic Job join, exact stdio-only handle inheritance, launch-state irreversibility, same-name Job fail-closed behavior and append-only fenced adoption with a mutable CAS head.
preconditions: Environment and Authorization identities are fixed

out: Interactive deletion of unknown Job objects

acceptance: CREATE_SUSPENDED, PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_JOB_LIST and PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_HANDLE_LIST are mandatory for every writer and StageJob process
STARTF_USESTDHANDLES is set and hStdInput/hStdOutput/hStdError exactly match the three role-correct child handles in the non-empty HANDLE_LIST while bInheritHandles is TRUE
Controller pipe ends and every Job, mutex, process/thread, DB, auth, policy, evidence, backup, quarantine, file and directory handle remain non-inheritable and absent from HANDLE_LIST
ChildHandleManifest binds role, object type, direction, access, inheritability read-back, ownership, attribute-list digest and expected EOF without persisting raw handle values
The controller closes its copies of child pipe ends before ResumeThread, and normal exit, kill, controller crash and response loss all reach bounded stdout/stderr EOF
Every process-bearing gate, Git, probe and recovery helper has a StageLaunchRecord, suspended atomic Job join and pre-resume execution commit
A new stage run proves the previous JobIdentity terminal, zero-process and fully drained before launch
writer_execution_committed is flushed before ResumeThread
No writer can restart after writer_execution_committed
Every StageLaunchRecord, FencedActionResult and terminal result references exactly one execution_authority_kind

Inherited fenced-action grants bind the parent action grant, current head, exact StageJob and next checkpoint and are forbidden for writer, GateRun, model decision, qualification, auth refresh and arbitrary commands
Safety-only records are the only authority for exact terminate/drain/seal/reconcile/handoff and emergency-reserve release/rebuild effects
release_emergency_reserve and rebuild_emergency_reserve are fenced, adoptable only for the same immutable safety record and exact reserve generation
First adoption points to intent hash and every later adoption points to the prior head
Same-name Job ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS always parks after closing the inspection handle
work_items.rollback: Revert only execution-safety artifacts and manifest references.
stop_conditions: A crash window permits a second writer
An adoption can fork from one prior head
prohibited_actions: Do not reuse or rename around a surviving Job
Do not make immutable intent mutable

work_items.title: Close event, journal, artifact, receipt and backup contracts
work_items.goal: Define secret-safe append-only evidence, runtime-managed external evidence, segment recovery, six-condition receipts, no-replace artifacts and anti-rollback state/evidence backup and restore.
preconditions: Launch and fence identities are fixed
acceptance: Every event/status combination and required/forbidden field set is explicit
Pre-scan events contain only random observation IDs, counts, approved path IDs and classes
Journal DB cursor can only lag the flushed journal
ExecutionReceipt requires all six terminal conditions
runtime_external_v1 evidence is activation-bound, controller-published and identity/ancestry/alias-disjoint from repo, Git, worktree, attempt and controller-sensitive roots
Quarantine and runtime-integrity keys use independent purpose-bound current-user DPAPI envelopes, and required envelopes are included without plaintext key material
Backups exclude credentials, worktrees and nonterminal state
Only an already-suspended backup with unchanged control generation can publish an eligible restore head; ordinary online backups are restore-drill-only

Every later authoritative mutation writes and flushes BackupPostActivity before staling the eligible head, and production restore uses one immutable BackupRestoreIntent plus one eligible-to-restoring-to-consumed CAS chain
Missing sidecars, post-backup activity, copied old backup directories, generation drift or ambiguous response loss fail closed with no force or marker-deletion path
work_items.rollback: Revert only evidence artifacts and manifest references.
stop_conditions: Any pre-scan field acts as a low-entropy oracle
A receipt can be emitted before Job zero-process or EOF
A stale or copied backup can regain production-restore eligibility
prohibited_actions: Do not persist raw process output or its content hash
Do not mutate prior events

work_items.title: Close deterministic Git publication contracts
work_items.goal: Define audited Git environment, attempt-local object creation, canonical object promotion, worktree finalization, task-ref publication and cleanup ordering.
preconditions: Evidence and fenced-action conventions are fixed
acceptance: Commit has exactly one expected parent and fixed header order
claim_epoch_seconds is generated once by claim CAS and bound by ResolvedAttemptManifest and GitObjectPlan
Existing object verification uses canonical type, size and payload/OID, never loose zlib bytes
Publication order is promote, reachability, index, HEAD, task-ref, evidence, remove
create_worktree removes only its own per-worktree logs/HEAD and HEAD/task-ref updates use --no-create-reflog with absence proofs
finalize_worktree proves path/mode/bytes equality before metadata updates
Task ref uses zero-OID CAS and historical success is durable
work_items.rollback: Revert only deterministic Git artifacts and manifest references.
stop_conditions: Any Git call can inherit uncontrolled config
Task ref can precede finalized HEAD/index
prohibited_actions: Do not run Git against a real target repo during contract authoring
Do not use reset --hard

work_items.title: Materialize complete state, guard, autonomy and operator catalogs
work_items.goal: Write every legal transition row for submission families, tasks, attempts, platform lanes, repo maintenance/cutover, templates, autonomy and operator actions.
preconditions: All domain contracts referenced by guards are fixed
acceptance: Every row has source, operation/event, guards, allowed effects, target, exit, capacity, priority and retry policy
Unknown combinations exit 2
Guard dependency graph is acyclic and has fixed precedence
Recovery-eligible attempts outrank new work
Global resume cannot clear repo/template qualification blocks
work_items.rollback: Revert only state/guard/operator catalogs and manifest references.
stop_conditions: A legal CLI operation has no transition row
Guard dependencies contain a cycle
prohibited_actions: Do not encode transition policy only in prose
Do not merge independent state domains

work_items.title: Close Q0, gate, feature and resource-limit catalogs
work_items.goal: Materialize Full/quick/daily qualification matrices, exact offline gates, effective feature inventory and all resource boundary fixtures.
preconditions: State and guard scopes are fixed
acceptance: Every gate stores exact executable, argv, env digest, cwd, timeout and expected report
Every sandbox gate/Git argv includes --include-managed-config

ProcessEnvironmentPolicy uses OrdinalIgnoreCase uniqueness for ASCII catalog keys, rejects aliases, hidden =X: entries and NUL, emits one sorted UTF-16 block with an exact double-NUL terminator and verifies the child-observed block before resume
Q0 proves JOB_LIST plus HANDLE_LIST atomic spawn, exact STARTF_USESTDHANDLES stdio roles, no ambient sensitive handles and bounded EOF after exit, kill, crash and response loss
Q0 proves runtime-managed external evidence root isolation, purpose-separated DPAPI key envelopes and suspended-only single-consumption restore eligibility
Every bounded field has limit-1/limit/limit+1 fixtures
Write limits cover single file, aggregate changed bytes, gate cache/output, attempt growth and object-store promotion
Mandatory accounting_kill_audit binds pre-spawn snapshots, continuous watcher, no slower than 500 ms fallback, durable limit+1 kill intent and final no-follow audit
A controller-only fully allocated 1 GiB EmergencyDiskReserve has fenced release/rebuild lifecycle and release leaves the platform suspended
HardWriteQuotaCapability is optional and enabled only after Full Q0 proves an applicable per-root or per-sandbox-user primitive
Low disk enters disk_pressure/resource_exhausted and never needs_environment
Unknown effective capability fails incompatible
Q0 covers sandbox mutable/secret state identity, opaque sandbox diagnostic behavior and alias-aware NTFS 8.3 handle identity
Q0 triggers include binary/model/profile/policy/adapter/environment/schema changes
Failure scope distinguishes attempt, template, repo and platform
work_items.rollback: Revert only Q0/gate/limit artifacts and manifest references.
stop_conditions: A gate command contains a shell placeholder or ellipsis
An effective capability is uncataloged
prohibited_actions: Do not run a live Q0 while authoring the contract
Do not broaden failure scope without a guard reason

work_items.title: Assemble migration specifications, cross-contract examples and negative fixtures
work_items.goal: Define persistence migrations and create a closed example inventory covering happy paths, response loss, crash windows, invalid states, secret-safe evidence and limit boundaries across every normative schema.
preconditions: All normative schemas and catalogs are present

in: Negative fixtures with expected reason codes
Forward migration, rollback, compatibility window and crash fixtures
out: Runtime simulations presented as live evidence

acceptance: Every Tier A schema has at least one positive and one negative example
Every crash boundary has a pre/post fixture
Every expected rejection carries a stable reason code
Fixture manifest has no dangling paths or duplicate IDs
Every persistence schema change has forward, rollback, compatibility and interrupted-migration fixtures

Fixtures cover exact stdio HANDLE_LIST/STARTF mappings, parent-end close and EOF, environment case aliases/hidden entries, external-evidence root overlap, wrong-purpose key unwrap, post-backup mutation and restore-intent response loss
work_items.rollback: Revert only examples/fixtures and their manifest references.
stop_conditions: A normative schema has no executable example
A negative fixture has implementation-dependent expected behavior
prohibited_actions: Do not label fixtures as implementation evidence
Do not omit limit+1 cases

work_items.title: Implement the standalone normative package verifier
work_items.goal: Implement a fail-closed verifier for bytes, manifest closure, schemas, catalogs, transitions, guards, examples, fixtures and hash references.
preconditions: All required normative artifacts are available
acceptance: Verifier output is stable JSON and exit 0 only for a closed package
Tampering any byte, hash, schema, transition or fixture fails
Missing, extra and cyclic references fail
Verifier never rewrites input
All package inventory entries become present only after verification
work_items.rollback: Revert only the verifier, its tests and package inventory updates.
stop_conditions: Verifier depends on network or unpinned packages
Verifier can pass an incomplete package
prohibited_actions: Do not create approval records
Do not auto-fix malformed artifacts

work_items.title: Freeze the review head, create the final manifest and close consistency review
work_items.goal: Build ReviewEvidenceIndex.v1, run the preliminary consistency review, freeze package_review_head, create the one-time final BaselineManifest, append the manifest-closure review and produce an approval-ready package without self-approving it.
preconditions: Standalone baseline verifier passes
All artifacts except P0A-MANIFEST and P0A-REVIEW are present

in: Frozen package_review_head and one-time final BaselineManifest.v1 instance
Manifest-closure review and approval_review_head successor proof

acceptance: Preliminary review covers every narrative clause, schema, catalog, transition row, example, fixture and verifier and freezes package_review_head
Final BaselineManifest is created exactly once after every other normative artifact is present and binds package_review_head without self-reference
Manifest-closure review is appended after manifest creation and proves bytes, dependency closure, artifact versions and verifier results
Review index hash chain verifies and approval_review_head is a successor of package_review_head
No P0 or P1 normative finding remains open
P0A-MANIFEST and P0A-REVIEW become present and package approval_eligible becomes true only after the final verifier and closure review are green
Approval remains inactive until a separately authorized BaselineApprovalRecord is written
work_items.rollback: Before publication, remove only an unaccepted manifest attempt; after review acceptance, append a correction/revocation entry and issue a new baseline/artifact version rather than rewriting accepted bytes.
stop_conditions: Any blocking finding remains
Review evidence and baseline manifest hashes disagree
Creating the manifest would require changing an already-present normative artifact
prohibited_actions: Do not modify prior review records or any present normative artifact
Do not create the final manifest before package_review_head is frozen
Do not issue approval without explicit authority

work_items.title: Authorize or reject the v3.21 baseline
work_items.goal: After package closure, require an explicitly authorized operator to create the immutable BaselineApprovalRecord or reject the candidate without changing normative bytes.
preconditions: Normative package is complete and approval_eligible
Standalone verifier and consistency review are green
No active approval or unresolved revocation exists
The approving operator has explicit authority

in: Approved review head and manifest hash

acceptance: Approval record binds baseline bytes, BaselineManifest, review head, approver identity hash, generation and UTC time
Active approval map advances by expected-generation CAS
Repeated approval response loss returns the same record
A rejection leaves approval inactive and requires a new baseline version for semantic changes
No Truth Reset or implementation side effect occurs
work_items.rollback: Never delete or edit an approval; append BaselineApprovalRevocationRecord or SupersessionRecord and CAS the active generation.
stop_conditions: Explicit approval authority is absent
Any package/review hash differs
A blocking finding is open
prohibited_actions: AI must not self-authorize approval
Do not modify normative artifacts
Do not perform Truth Reset in the approval transaction

work_items.title: Perform the approved Truth Reset
work_items.goal: After active Baseline Approval, atomically make the approved v3.21 package identity the implementation source of truth across root rules, docs, planning status, selector and verifier.
preconditions: Active, non-revoked BaselineApprovalRecord exists
Approved baseline and manifest hashes verify
acceptance: Queue becomes MINIMUM-OPERATOR-COMMIT-ONLY-TRANSITION
Selector returns implement_legacy_guard_first
Existing host-orchestrator remains current executable truth until cutover
No document claims new runtime implementation exists
work_items.rollback: Revert the Truth Reset change set only; do not revoke the approved baseline unless its semantics are invalid.
stop_conditions: Approval is absent, revoked or hash-mismatched
Any authoritative surface still selects the old implementation queue
prohibited_actions: Do not start new runtime code
Do not alter .ai/config or live state

work_items.title: Implement ownership wire contract and legacy entrypoint inventory
work_items.goal: Create the shared repo ownership wire contract and enumerate every legacy path capable of claim, worktree, writer, Git, closeout or cleanup side effects.
preconditions: Truth Reset is complete
acceptance: Legacy and new runtimes compute identical repo mutex names
Existing repositories are registered legacy-owned without mutation
Unknown/corrupt ownership fails closed
Inventory covers all mutation-capable legacy entrypoints
work_items.rollback: Revert ownership adapter code and leave preexisting repos legacy-owned.
stop_conditions: Any mutation entrypoint cannot be identified
Repo identity differs across adapters
prohibited_actions: Do not enable new Batch claims
Do not migrate legacy DB

work_items.title: Guard every legacy writer and repository side-effect path
work_items.goal: Require valid ownership generation and repo mutex acquisition before every legacy side effect, with fail-closed tests for stale or mismatched ownership.
preconditions: Legacy entrypoint inventory is complete
acceptance: No inventoried side-effect path bypasses ownership
Generation drift blocks before side effect
Cross-process mutex tests prove serialization
Legacy behavior is unchanged when validly legacy-owned
work_items.rollback: Revert the guard wiring as one slice; retain the ownership schema only if it remains non-enforcing.
stop_conditions: Any existing test regresses without an approved contract change
A guard is checked after a side effect
prohibited_actions: Do not weaken legacy evidence contracts
Do not dual-write databases

work_items.title: Prove legacy/new ownership conformance and rollback
work_items.goal: Add cross-runtime wire, crash, takeover, cutover and rollback drills before any new runtime can claim a repository.
preconditions: All legacy side-effect paths are guarded
acceptance: Legacy/new names, bytes and generations match
Concurrent claims produce one owner
Crash windows never permit two writers
Rollback restores guarded legacy ownership without deleting evidence
work_items.rollback: Revert only conformance harness changes; keep runtime claims disabled.
stop_conditions: Any double-owner trace appears
Rollback requires destructive state rewrite
prohibited_actions: Do not enable scheduler claims
Do not call this live acceptance

work_items.title: Create the isolated local-ai-runtime package and acceptance harness
work_items.goal: Create the pinned Python 3.11 modular-monolith skeleton, offline lock/build/test/lint/typecheck harness and contract CLI without importing host_orchestrator.
preconditions: Legacy/new ownership conformance is green
Baseline Approval remains active
acceptance: Offline build and frozen tests pass
Import scan proves no host_orchestrator dependency
contracts verify exposes stable JSON
No executable Batch path exists yet
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime ruff check runtime/local-ai-runtime
work_items.rollback: Remove only the new package skeleton and its planning/evidence references.
stop_conditions: Offline dependency resolution is not reproducible
New package imports legacy implementation
prohibited_actions: Do not copy legacy runtime code wholesale
Do not activate the package

work_items.title: Implement canonical bytes, hashes and path primitives
work_items.goal: Implement only the approved canonical JSON envelope, normative byte validator, domain-separated hashing, Git path validator and Windows collision/identity primitives.
preconditions: Package skeleton gates pass
Approved canonicalization artifacts and fixtures are available read-only

in: Canonical JSON parse/serialize and duplicate-key rejection
Git path, Windows collision key and alias-aware identity primitives

acceptance: Canonical serialization preserves ordinary array order and rejects duplicate JSON keys and duplicate set keys
Every approved byte, Unicode, Git path, NTFS collision and alias fixture returns the cataloged reason code
Domain separators make equal payloads of different artifact types hash differently
No function rewrites Git spelling, source bytes or already-present normative artifacts
work_items.rollback: Revert only canonical.py, paths.py, their tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A fixture requires locale-dependent behavior
A required Windows identity primitive cannot be tested without broadening privileges
prohibited_actions: Do not normalize input bytes or Git path spelling
Do not add storage, CLI or process behavior

work_items.title: Implement the schema registry and typed contract models
work_items.goal: Load approved schemas and catalogs as immutable resources and expose typed models without inventing implementation-only fields or defaults.
preconditions: Canonical primitives pass
Every referenced artifact ID/version/hash exists in the approved BaselineManifest

in: Typed Tier A and Tier B contract models

acceptance: Registry rejects unknown artifact versions, hash drift, duplicate IDs and undeclared defaults
All approved positive examples round-trip to identical canonical bytes
All approved negative fixtures fail with their exact cataloged reason codes
Nullable, required and forbidden fields are enforced per event and record schema
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify --scope models
work_items.rollback: Revert only registry/models, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: Implementation requires a field absent from an approved schema
Generated or parsed output is nondeterministic
prohibited_actions: Do not patch approved schemas or examples
Do not add permissive unknown-field handling

work_items.title: Implement state, guard and operator catalog evaluation
work_items.goal: Evaluate the approved SubmissionFamily, BatchTask, Attempt, Platform, Repo, Template, Autonomy and OperatorAction transition rows with deterministic guard precedence.
preconditions: Typed models and catalog registry pass

in: Guard precedence, scope and reason-code resolution
Capacity disposition and scheduler priority outputs

acceptance: Every approved source/operation/guard combination resolves to exactly one transition row
Unknown state, operation, resolution, guard or illegal cross-domain dependency exits 2 without side effects
Guard dependency graph is acyclic and precedence is stable
Safety-only guards cannot authorize ordinary execution effects
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify --scope state
work_items.rollback: Revert only state/guard evaluators, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: Two rows can match the same input
A cross-domain guard cycle exists
prohibited_actions: Do not hard-code hidden transition branches
Do not mutate state from the evaluator

work_items.title: Implement cross-contract policy bundle verification
work_items.goal: Bind artifact generations into one immutable policy bundle and mechanically verify cross-contract references, limits, action allowlists and execution-authority unions.
preconditions: Canonical, model and state evaluators pass

in: Policy bundle loading and generation identity

acceptance: Every foreign artifact/catalog/reason/guard/action ID resolves at the declared version and hash
Every StageLaunchRecord and terminal result schema admits exactly one approved execution authority kind
All bounded fields have limit-1, limit and limit+1 fixtures
The complete approved package passes and every tamper fixture fails closed
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify
work_items.rollback: Revert only policy-bundle verification, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A cross-contract reference is ambiguous
Verifier acceptance depends on artifact load order
prohibited_actions: Do not add implicit compatibility fallbacks
Do not modify normative artifacts

work_items.title: Implement SQLite bootstrap and schema migrations
work_items.goal: Create the isolated runtime database with approved pragmas, initial tables, foreign keys and reversible versioned migrations without touching the legacy control plane.
preconditions: Cross-contract policy bundle passes
PersistenceMigrationSpecification is approved and read-only

in: Initial approved schema and migration ledger

acceptance: Connections enforce journal_mode=DELETE, foreign_keys=ON, synchronous=FULL and busy_timeout=5000
Fresh create, each forward step, rollback and interrupted migration are deterministic in isolated roots
Schema contains every approved table/index/unique constraint and no undeclared table
The code refuses .ai/state/control-plane.db and any path outside the supplied isolated runtime root
work_items.rollback: Revert only database bootstrap/migrations and delete only test databases in isolated temporary roots.
stop_conditions: A migration cannot round-trip
An approved uniqueness or foreign-key invariant cannot be represented
prohibited_actions: Do not touch .ai/state control planes
Do not share or migrate a DB with host-orchestrator

work_items.title: Implement submission-family and resubmission transactions
work_items.goal: Implement permanent ordinary-submit replay and the one-transaction TaskResubmission relation with response-loss idempotency.
preconditions: Database bootstrap and migration tests pass
Product/submission schemas and secret-safe admission ordering are approved

in: Volatile existing-family lookup and authorized replay

acceptance: Ordinary duplicate submit always returns immutable generation-0 root_task_id even after resubmission
Existing-family replay occurs before current catalog/secret/qualification admission and persists no new oracle
One source task creates at most one successor across response loss and future resolution code versions
Historical task-ref success, stale base, non-current source and nonterminal source fail with exact reason codes when no prior successor exists
work_items.rollback: Revert only submission repositories/service, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A response-loss path can create a second family or successor
Rejected absent-family input leaves parameters, digest or stable oracle
prohibited_actions: Do not add ResubmissionPermit
Do not revalidate current admission before returning an existing authorized family

work_items.title: Implement leases, fences and execution-authority CAS
work_items.goal: Persist capacity/repo/attempt leases, fence generations, immutable action/authority records and mutable heads with one deterministic lock and transaction order.
preconditions: Submission transactions pass
Execution authority and fenced-action schemas are approved
acceptance: Lease takeover requires expected generation/fence CAS and cannot regress generation
Root grant and revoke transactions use the approved global lock order and serialize on the same active map
Each effect has exactly one authority kind and each action prior head has at most one adoption
Terminal result is unique and binds current head kind/hash/fence/version
work_items.rollback: Revert only lease/fenced-action repositories, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A head can fork or a terminal result can duplicate
Grant and revoke can acquire locks in opposite order
prohibited_actions: Do not mutate immutable intent, adoption, authority or result rows
Do not implement OS side effects in storage repositories

work_items.title: Implement event, journal, outbox and artifact cursors
work_items.goal: Persist append-only normalized event metadata, journal segment cursors, artifact/outbox intents and terminal receipts while keeping raw output and secret oracles out of the database.
preconditions: Lease/fence/authority CAS tests pass
Evidence and journal schemas are approved
acceptance: DB accepted offset can lag but never lead a flushed journal segment
Events are append-only and retain no raw stdout/stderr, unknown path digest or unscanned content hash
Outbox logical name and terminal result uniqueness survive response loss
Random quarantine IDs are independent of path and content
work_items.rollback: Revert only journal/outbox repositories, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A DB transaction can acknowledge unflushed bytes
A schema requires storing prohibited raw output or digest
prohibited_actions: Do not parse process output in the storage layer
Do not publish artifacts or evidence

work_items.title: Prove persistence crash recovery and response-loss replay
work_items.goal: Run the storage-level crash matrix across migrations, CAS, family creation, resubmission, grants, adoption, journal cursors and outbox terminalization.
preconditions: All P1B repositories and focused tests pass
acceptance: Every approved storage failpoint reopens to one valid pre-effect or post-effect state
Response loss returns the same family, task, successor, grant, action and outbox IDs
Integrity audit detects every tampered head, cursor, checksum and generation fixture
Test teardown deletes only isolated temporary roots
work_items.rollback: Revert only failpoint harness/tests/evidence; retain the already-verified P1B storage implementation.
stop_conditions: Any failpoint produces an ambiguous or unverifiable state
A replay depends on wall-clock timing
prohibited_actions: Do not inject failures into legacy or live databases
Do not weaken synchronous or foreign-key settings for test speed

work_items.title: Implement immutable installation and activation rollback
work_items.goal: Install content-addressed runtime versions and atomically activate or roll back current.json with generation, checksum, flush and schema-compatibility guards in isolated roots.
preconditions: P1B crash and response-loss matrix passes
RuntimeActivationRecord and filesystem atomicity contracts are approved

in: Compatible rollback and activation crash probes

acceptance: Install is content-addressed and immutable
Activation uses expected generation/checksum and atomic replace
Rollback never crosses schema incompatibility
Every install/replace/flush failpoint resolves to the previous or new verified generation
work_items.rollback: Revert only installer/activation code and delete only isolated test roots and versions created by this task.
stop_conditions: Activation cannot be crash-tested
The filesystem cannot prove the approved replace/flush behavior
prohibited_actions: Do not activate into the production LOCALAPPDATA root
Do not create or modify provider credentials

work_items.title: Implement toolchain and qualified environment bindings
work_items.goal: Discover and verify pinned executable/file identities and immutable dependency environments without installing, restoring or bootstrapping during Batch.
preconditions: Installation and activation crash tests pass
Toolchain/environment schemas and absolute command contracts are approved

in: Absolute executable/PATH and offline dependency checks

acceptance: Every executable and wrapper binds absolute final path, file identity, version and SHA-256
Environment roots are immutable, no-follow verified and disjoint from writable attempt roots
Unknown, missing or drifted tools fail with scoped catalog reason codes
Verification performs no package install, restore, network access or production activation
work_items.rollback: Revert only toolchain/environment qualification code, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A required identity cannot be reproduced without network or install side effects
Environment mutability cannot be excluded
prohibited_actions: Do not install dependencies
Do not trust PATH lookup without final identity verification

work_items.title: Implement repo/template qualification, auth state and Authorization
work_items.goal: Recompute qualification-sensitive input closure, register repo/template qualification, track keyring-only global AuthState and activate/revoke Authorizations with exact generation bindings.
preconditions: Toolchain and environment bindings pass
Qualification resolver/catalog and Authorization contracts are approved

in: Keyring-only AuthState classification without credential persistence

acceptance: QualificationObservation is independently reproducible from the current expected base tree, resolver catalog, negative discovery and alias probes
The reusable sensitive-set fingerprint excludes ordinary base commit/tree identity, and a byte-identical set refreshes observation without invalidating qualification or Authorization
Dirty, staged, untracked, blocked external, collision or ambiguous discovery inputs fail closed
Authorization binds every approved generation and revoke serializes with root effect grants
AuthState stores only generation/store/status/qualified time and needs_auth stops all new provider claims
work_items.rollback: Revert only repo/template/auth/Authorization qualification code, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: Qualification input closure is ambiguous
A test requires reading or persisting real credentials
prohibited_actions: Do not perform login/logout
Do not hash dirty working-tree content into Authorization

work_items.title: Implement Codex sandbox state and effective-config qualification
work_items.goal: Compile the pinned untrusted-project overlay, verify immutable/mutable/secret sandbox roots and treat sandbox diagnostics as opaque bounded state.
preconditions: Repo/template/Auth qualification passes
Codex feature, environment and sandbox-state policies are approved

in: Effective config and model-visible tool inventory verification

acceptance: Effective config always contains projects.<canonical-worktree>.trust_level=untrusted
Unknown effective feature/tool or opaque sandbox entry triggers the approved scoped failure
Task process access to sandbox mutable/secret roots is denied by the generated policy
sandbox.log content and content hash never enter ordinary records, evidence or backup
work_items.rollback: Revert only overlay/sandbox-state code, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: Effective behavior cannot be bound to pinned input and probe IDs
Sandbox secrets or opaque log bytes would enter a durable ordinary record
prohibited_actions: Do not parse undocumented sandbox.log fields
Do not run live Codex or export diagnostics

work_items.title: Implement write accounting and emergency reserve lifecycle
work_items.goal: Implement mandatory accounting_kill_audit, immutable snapshots, disk-pressure classification and the fenced 1 GiB controller-only emergency reserve; keep hard quota optional.
preconditions: Sandbox state qualification passes
ResourceLimitPolicy and emergency-reserve contracts are approved

in: No-follow logical/allocated byte and entry-count snapshots
ReadDirectoryChangesW watcher plus bounded monotonic fallback

acceptance: Snapshots bind root identities, logical/allocated bytes, entry count, free-space floor and reserve generation
Fallback recomputation interval never exceeds 500 ms and final audit detects missed notifications
Reserve is non-sparse, non-compressed, fully allocated, task-inaccessible and released only by exact safety action
Release keeps platform suspended; rebuild requires zero active attempts and advances generation by CAS
Hard quota remains disabled unless an applicable primitive passes the complete Q0 bypass matrix
work_items.rollback: Revert only accounting/reserve code and tests; delete only task-created reserve files in isolated test roots after identity verification.
stop_conditions: A test can release or rebuild a reserve without exact identity/generation
Implementation claims atomic hard quota without a qualified primitive
prohibited_actions: Do not create the production reserve
Do not treat post-run scanning as pre-effect denial

work_items.title: Implement Full, quick and daily qualification probes
work_items.goal: Run exact offline Full/quick/daily probe matrices in isolated fixtures and emit scoped immutable reports without performing production activation or provider login.
preconditions: All P1C qualification components pass
QualificationGateCatalog declares every probe ID, input, expected report and failure scope

in: Alias, sandbox, network, Job, filesystem, accounting and reserve probes

acceptance: Every required probe executes once in deterministic catalog order with exact timeout and input identity
Reports bind toolchain/environment/policy/probe generations and contain no credential, raw output or opaque log content hash
Failure scope distinguishes attempt, template, repo and platform according to GuardCatalog
Missing, unknown or behavior-drifted probes fail closed
work_items.rollback: Revert only probe/report harness, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A probe requires live credentials or unapproved network
A failure cannot be assigned an approved scope
prohibited_actions: Do not create a FullQ0Approval or P2Admission record
Do not mutate production platform state

work_items.title: Implement named-object identity and empty Job lifecycle
work_items.goal: Create and verify global capacity, repo, attempt and Job named objects with canonical names, exact SDDL, limits and same-name fail-closed handling before launching any payload.
preconditions: P1C offline qualification harness passes
Named-object, JobIdentity and ownership contracts are approved
acceptance: Every name exactly matches the approved SID/repo/attempt hash grammar
Opened objects verify type, owner, DACL and Job limits before use
Inspection handle is closed while attempt mutex remains held before CreateJobObject
ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS closes the returned handle and parks without rename, reuse or process launch
Tests terminate only processes proven runtime-owned by executable hash, PID and creation time
work_items.rollback: Revert only named-object/Job helper code; close only handles created by tests and terminate only identity-proven test processes.
stop_conditions: An object cannot be fully verified through its handle
Test cleanup cannot prove process ownership
prohibited_actions: Do not kill unknown processes
Do not launch or resume a writer, gate or Git process

work_items.title: Implement writer marker and atomic suspended launch
work_items.goal: Implement the writer launch-intent, durable CREATE_NEW marker, atomic Job/handle-list spawn, exact environment and stdio inheritance, persisted PID identity and pre-ResumeThread execution-commit barrier.
preconditions: Named-object and empty Job tests pass
Writer launch state and AttemptProcessEnvironment contracts are approved

in: Attempt-local environment materialization and identity recheck
CREATE_SUSPENDED plus PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_JOB_LIST and PROC_THREAD_ATTRIBUTE_HANDLE_LIST launch
ProcessHandlePolicy, ChildHandleManifest and exact STARTF_USESTDHANDLES stdio mapping

acceptance: Marker uses CREATE_NEW, write-through, flush and exact task/attempt/fence/intent checksum
Environment construction rejects case-insensitive aliases, hidden =X: entries, duplicate/invalid keys and NUL, then serializes the canonical sorted UTF-16 block with an exact final double NUL
Child starts suspended and is atomically assigned to the pre-created no-breakaway Job
The only inherited handles are the role-correct stdin/stdout/stderr child ends named by STARTF_USESTDHANDLES and HANDLE_LIST; all controller-sensitive handles remain non-inheritable
Controller copies of child pipe ends close before resume and ChildHandleManifest read-back proves handle type, access, ownership and expected EOF
PID/creation time/executable/authority are durable before writer_execution_committed
writer_execution_committed is durable before ResumeThread and permanently blocks another writer for that task generation
Every marker/spawn/barrier/resume response-loss failpoint has one allowed recovery state
work_items.rollback: Revert only writer-launch/environment code; terminate and remove only identity-proven suspended test Jobs and roots.
stop_conditions: A child can execute before durable writer_execution_committed
A crash permits a second writer for one task generation
prohibited_actions: Do not run live Codex
Do not infer non-execution from zero tool or mutation events

work_items.title: Implement StageJobs and execution-authority enforcement
work_items.goal: Launch gate, Git, probe and recovery helpers through suspended StageJobs with exact environment/stdio handle inheritance while enforcing the closed root, inherited-fenced-action and safety-only authority union.
preconditions: Writer launch crash matrix passes
AuthorizationExecutionGrant and SafetyOnlyExecutionRecord persistence passes

in: Safety-only helper authority and durable kill intent

acceptance: Each StageLaunchRecord references exactly one authority kind/ID/hash and one JobIdentity
Every StageJob uses JOB_LIST plus a non-empty exact HANDLE_LIST, STARTF_USESTDHANDLES and no ambient inherited controller/auth/DB/evidence handle
Each run closes parent copies of child ends before resume and reaches EOF or a terminal recovery disposition without reusing the process identity
Inherited grant binds parent action grant, current fenced head, exact argv/StageJob and next checkpoint
Writer, GateRun, model decision, qualification, auth refresh and arbitrary commands reject inherited basis
Safety-only helper effects are limited to exact terminate/drain/seal/reconcile/handoff/reserve operations
limit+1 and emergency kill persist operator/action/safety/terminate intent before Job termination
work_items.rollback: Revert only StageJob/authority enforcement and focused tests; terminate only identity-proven test Jobs.
stop_conditions: A process can run without exactly one execution authority
Inherited or safety authority can widen the logical effect
prohibited_actions: Do not add an uncataloged authority kind
Do not use Authorization expiry to block required safety closeout

work_items.title: Implement bounded stream draining and normalized journals
work_items.goal: Continuously drain stdout/stderr without deadlock, parse complete JSONL into append-only secret-safe events and seal hash-linked journal segments.
preconditions: StageJob authority tests pass
Event matrix, reason catalog and JournalSegmentManifest are approved

in: Bounded JSONL framing and adapter validation
Normalized event hash chain and segment seal

acceptance: Readers continuously drain both pipes and close the Job only after durable limit+1 intent
Reader completion requires the ChildHandleManifest expected EOF condition and detects an unexpected surviving inheritor without waiting unboundedly
Raw output text and content hash are discarded; durable records keep only approved counts/codes/reports
Complete invalid lines classify adapter incompatibility while abnormal EOF partial buffers classify attempt recovery
Pre-scan unknown paths store no path/content digest; validated events append rather than mutate earlier events
DB cursor never leads the flushed segment and continuation preserves prev_hash/sequence
work_items.rollback: Revert only stream/journal code, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A pipe can deadlock or grow unbounded
A durable record would retain prohibited raw output or pre-scan digest
prohibited_actions: Do not rewrite accepted events or truncate corrupt journals
Do not classify all partial EOF as platform incompatible

work_items.title: Implement adoption, continuation and recovery handoff
work_items.goal: Recover crashed controller attempts through immutable adoption chains, Authorization continuation and checkpoint-specific recovery without replaying writer or ambiguous StageJobs.
preconditions: Stream/journal crash tests pass
Fenced action and continuation repositories pass

in: Controller takeover proof and fence CAS
Recovery eligibility, priority and capacity release
out: New writer launch after execution commit

acceptance: Takeover proves old mutex release, PID/creation death, TTL, Job state and fence CAS
Adoption can only advance the current head of an approved deterministic action and cannot fork
Continuation binds old/new Authorization, fence, manifest, marker/receipt, environment and exact allowed phase
writer_execution_committed permanently forbids another writer; ambiguous StageJob outcomes park
Recoverable attempts outrank new tasks and parked external waits release capacity
work_items.rollback: Revert only recovery orchestration/tests/evidence; preserve immutable test records for the evidence fixture set.
stop_conditions: A takeover cannot explain the prior Job/process outcome
A continuation can authorize writer or an undeclared phase
prohibited_actions: Do not mutate prior intent/adoption/continuation records
Do not infer non-execution from missing events

work_items.title: Implement the exact offline gate runner
work_items.goal: Execute only cataloged absolute gate commands through authorized StageJobs and produce bounded schema-whitelisted GateReports with deterministic timeout/cancel behavior.
preconditions: Recovery/adoption matrix passes
GatePolicy contains exact executable/argv/env/cwd/timeout/report contracts

in: Bounded report parsing and aggregate deadlines

acceptance: Only exact catalog executable/argv/environment/cwd combinations run and all include managed config as required
Gate stdout/stderr body and content hash are discarded after bounded report extraction
Per-gate, aggregate and attempt deadlines enforce the approved limit and limit+1 outcomes
Unknown report fields, shell placeholders, network attempts and environment drift fail closed with scoped reason codes
work_items.rollback: Revert only gate runner/report code, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A gate requires shell interpolation or undeclared environment
A report cannot be validated without retaining raw output
prohibited_actions: Do not run gates against a registered real repository
Do not add dynamic command fallback

work_items.title: Implement hardened Git identity and preflight audit
work_items.goal: Resolve the original repository/common-dir by no-follow handles and reject every unapproved Git config, attribute, hook, driver, ref, object-format and protected-surface input before any worktree or object write.
preconditions: P1D gate runner and recovery invariants pass
GitConfigPolicy, RepoProfile and protected-surface catalogs are approved

in: Fixed Git environment and config audit

acceptance: First Git invocation is the exact sandboxed audit command with absolute executable/argv and fixed environment
Git common/admin/worktree identities are no-follow verified and cannot overlap forbidden runtime/evidence roots
Unknown config, include, filter, driver, hook, attribute, exclude, alternate, replacement, submodule/LFS or protocol surface fails closed
Only local fixed-NTFS SHA-1/files repositories with one exact local_base_ref pass
Audit performs no repository write and retains no sensitive config values
work_items.rollback: Revert only Git identity/audit code, focused tests and this task evidence; remove only temporary repos created by tests.
stop_conditions: A Git execution surface cannot be enumerated
An audit requires reading or storing credential-bearing values
prohibited_actions: Do not run against registered real repositories
Do not issue any Git write command

work_items.title: Implement fenced worktree setup and mutation closure
work_items.goal: Create/checkout the isolated worktree through adoptable controller actions, verify attempt roots and close the writer mutation set through path policy, protected-surface audit and secret scan.
preconditions: Git identity/preflight audit passes in temporary repositories
create_worktree and checkout_base action contracts are approved

in: Secret scan and approved changed-path closure

acceptance: Worktree and checkout actions are idempotent/adoptable only through current fenced head and exact authority
All writable roots and ancestors pass identity/ACL/reparse/hardlink checks before and after each process
Unknown, denied, collision, protected or secret-bearing mutations fail without storing path/content oracle
Accepted changed paths, modes and bytes form one immutable input/index plan
work_items.rollback: Revert only worktree/mutation code; remove only runtime-owned temporary worktrees after proving base-clean state and admin-record ownership.
stop_conditions: A worktree side effect cannot be assigned to one fenced action
Mutation closure cannot exclude an unknown or protected path
prohibited_actions: Do not use reset --hard
Do not write common Git objects or refs

work_items.title: Implement deterministic object planning and promotion
work_items.goal: Create blobs, trees and the single-parent commit in an attempt-local object store, seal GitObjectPlan/ObjectSetManifest and promote canonical objects without loose-byte assumptions.
preconditions: Mutation closure produces an immutable accepted input/index plan
Deterministic commit grammar and object action graph are approved

in: Fenced create-if-absent common-store promotion and reachability

acceptance: Commit has exact tree, one expected parent, author, committer order, identity, UTC claim time and message bytes with no extra header
ObjectSetManifest binds every object type, size, OID, canonical payload hash and reachability edge before common-store write
Existing objects are verified by canonical type/size/payload/OID rather than compressed loose bytes
Promotion is create-if-absent, fenced, response-loss idempotent and followed by clear-alternates reachability proof
work_items.rollback: Revert only object/commit implementation and tests; delete only isolated attempt stores and never delete promoted common objects.
stop_conditions: Commit bytes differ across identical isolated runs
An existing object cannot be canonically verified
prohibited_actions: Do not compare loose zlib file bytes
Do not update worktree HEAD or any ref

work_items.title: Implement finalize-index, finalize-HEAD and task-ref publication
work_items.goal: After common-store reachability, atomically finalize the real worktree index, then detached HEAD, then the exact zero-old-value task ref as three ordered fenced actions.
preconditions: Object plan, promotion and reachability tests pass
Expected tree/worktree equality proof is available

in: Temporary expected index and old-index hash CAS replace
Exact task-ref zero-OID CAS and durable history

acceptance: Index action runs only after exact working-tree equality and replaces by expected old-index hash
HEAD action runs only after index read-back and CASes expected base to expected commit
Task ref runs only after index, HEAD and common reachability and uses zero old OID
Each action is independently adoptable/idempotent and crash checkpoints never reorder effects
Any historical successful task-ref record permanently blocks resubmission even if the ref is externally deleted
work_items.rollback: Revert only finalize/ref code and tests; never delete a successfully created task ref automatically.
stop_conditions: A ref can appear before index and HEAD finalization
Extra worktree content can be hidden by finalization
prohibited_actions: Do not use reset --hard
Do not update target/base refs or delete task refs

work_items.title: Implement artifact, external evidence and receipt closeout
work_items.goal: Publish scanned immutable artifacts and runtime-managed runtime_external_v1 evidence after task-ref success, then issue ExecutionReceipt and CloseoutBundle only when all mechanical terminal conditions verify.
preconditions: Finalize/ref crash matrix passes
External evidence root is identity-qualified and disjoint from all forbidden roots

in: EvidenceProjectionAcceptance plus activation-bound runtime_external_v1 root qualification

acceptance: Artifact publish is fenced no-replace and existing final validates only for the same intent/hash/size
Evidence root identity/ancestry/ACL/alias is rechecked before every publish and task processes cannot access it

The evidence root is the activation-bound runtime-managed root or allocated descendant and is disjoint from repo, Git, worktree, attempt, state, authorization, ownership, backup, quarantine, reserve and temp roots
Receipt requires process exit, EOF, final schema, limits, complete journal seal and zero Job processes
Evidence contains no raw output, secret-bearing hash, opaque log content or commit/evidence content-addressing cycle
Every response-loss checkpoint returns the same artifact/evidence/receipt identity
work_items.rollback: Revert only artifact/evidence code and tests; never rewrite published immutable evidence or delete a task ref.
stop_conditions: Evidence root separation cannot be proven
Receipt can publish without all terminal conditions
prohibited_actions: Do not publish evidence before task-ref success
Do not persist raw process output or unconditional content hashes

work_items.title: Implement safe cleanup and anti-rollback backup/restore
work_items.goal: Remove only fully proven runtime-owned worktrees, create/verify state-and-evidence backups under global/repo locks, run isolated restore drills and implement the suspended-only single-consumption production-restore state machine without invoking it on live state.
preconditions: Artifact/evidence/receipt closeout passes
Cleanup and backup catalogs are approved

in: Fenced remove_worktree proof and admin-record verification
Quiescent state/evidence backup lock ordering and contents
BackupRestoreEligibility, BackupPostActivity and BackupRestoreIntent state machine
Restore verification in a new isolated root and production-restore core against a disposable production-like fixture
out: Uncontrolled production-root overwrite or live production restore

acceptance: Cleanup proves ownership, zero Job, current fence, terminal actions/evidence and clean expected tree before removal
Removal verifies both directory and common-dir worktree admin record disappearance
Unprovable cleanup parks and preserves worktree/task ref rather than hiding files
Backup locks every referenced repo in canonical identity order and contains only approved state/evidence/sealed terminal quarantine
Required DPAPI-wrapped key envelopes are copied and same-SID drills reject missing, wrong-purpose, wrong-generation or tampered envelopes
Only a backup captured under an unchanged pre-existing suspended control generation becomes eligible; every later authoritative mutation durably marks it stale before that mutation

Production-restore core accepts one immutable intent, atomically consumes eligible-to-restoring-to-consumed once, remains suspended and rejects marker deletion, stale copies, generation drift and response-loss duplication
Restore drill never overwrites production root and detects every tamper fixture
work_items.rollback: Revert only cleanup/backup code and tests; retain immutable evidence/task refs and delete only verified isolated restore roots.
stop_conditions: A cleanup path can delete an unproven file or admin record
Backup can race an active lease/outbox or include forbidden content
Any post-backup activity or copied sidecar state can regain production-restore eligibility
prohibited_actions: Do not delete task refs
Do not restore over production or back up auth/environment/worktrees

work_items.title: Implement the stable CLI kernel and JSON envelope
work_items.goal: Expose the approved command tree, strict argument parsing, stable JSON response envelope and exit/reason-code mapping without enabling scheduled or production side effects.
preconditions: P1E deterministic closeout, cleanup and backup drills pass
CLI command and reason-code catalogs are approved

in: Top-level command tree and strict parser
Read-only dependency injection for command handlers

acceptance: Every approved command/subcommand exists and every undeclared option exits 2
JSON responses have stable schema, reason code, evidence locator and at most one suggested command
Exit-code mapping exactly matches the approved catalog
Parsing and read-only status commands perform no external side effect
Raw argv, env, prompt and secrets never enter durable CLI diagnostics
work_items.rollback: Revert only CLI parser/output code, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A command or reason code lacks an approved catalog entry
A read-only command requires a mutation
prohibited_actions: Do not install the scheduler
Do not invoke runtime effects from parser/output tests

work_items.title: Implement Batch prepare, submit, status and recovery commands
work_items.goal: Wire Batch prepare/submit/drain/status/cancel/retry/reconcile/resolve/doctor handlers to the approved kernel while preserving permanent replay and state-policy guards.
preconditions: CLI parser/JSON contract passes
All handler dependencies expose tested service interfaces

in: Batch command handlers and service orchestration
Ordinary submit and create_resubmission_v1 replay output

acceptance: Ordinary submit replay always returns root_task_id and resolve response loss returns the same successor
Every mutation is preceded by the exact policy guard and state transition
Reconcile is read-only and retry cannot bypass writer_execution_committed or task-ref history
All blocked states return the cataloged exit/reason/evidence/suggested-command tuple
work_items.rollback: Revert only Batch/doctor handlers, focused tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A handler needs a hidden state transition
A replay path performs a new side effect
prohibited_actions: Do not run against registered real repositories
Do not add free-prompt Batch or dynamic fallback

work_items.title: Implement single-capacity scheduling and parking
work_items.goal: Select one due recovery or promoted task at a time, park external waits without consuming capacity and expose scheduler install definitions without installing them.
preconditions: Batch command handlers pass
Scheduler priority, retry and capacity dispositions are approved

in: Global capacity lease and recovery-first ordering
out: Installing or starting a scheduled task

acceptance: Global capacity never exceeds one and due recovery outranks every new claim
Parked auth/environment/Authorization waits release capacity and dedupe notifications by approved key
Scheduled runtime definition is Limited/Hidden/IgnoreNew/StartWhenAvailable with exact five-minute cadence and deadline
Scheduled command allowlist excludes login, prepare, repair, cutover, maintenance and kill-current
work_items.rollback: Revert only scheduler/definition code and tests; no scheduled task should exist from this slice.
stop_conditions: Two claims can hold capacity concurrently
A parked wait blocks unrelated repo work
prohibited_actions: Do not install or run a Windows scheduled task
Do not perform login/logout or environment preparation

work_items.title: Implement managed Native maintenance and emergency kill
work_items.goal: Provide the durable drain-to-maintenance Native launcher and a separate emergency kill-current path with intent-before-termination ordering and scoped requalification.
preconditions: Scheduler/capacity/parking tests pass
Repo maintenance and operator action transition tables are approved

in: Repo maintenance begin/end and managed Native launcher
Emergency kill-current durable intent and recovery handoff

acceptance: Normal maintenance stops new claims and waits for the active attempt; it never invokes kill-current
Native launch occurs only after durable repo maintenance under global capacity and repo mutex
Post-maintenance failure blocks only the affected repo/template while unaffected scheduling resumes
kill-current flushes OperatorWorkSession, OperatorAction, terminate intent and SafetyOnlyExecutionRecord before Job termination
A crash during emergency kill adopts the same terminate action and completes drain/seal/handoff
work_items.rollback: Revert only managed Native/maintenance code and tests; leave any durable test maintenance record for fixture verification and clean only isolated roots.
stop_conditions: Normal maintenance can terminate a writer
Emergency termination can occur before durable intent
prohibited_actions: Do not install global hooks or auto-release maintenance
Do not run Native against a registered real repository

work_items.title: Implement operator actions, runbooks and work-session metrics
work_items.goal: Render deduplicated operator actions with one safe command and provide exact runbooks for every external/manual recovery state while measuring net operator minutes without prompts or secrets.
preconditions: Managed Native and emergency-kill tests pass
OperatorActionCatalog and runbook inventory are approved
acceptance: Every operator state has one versioned reason, scope, dedupe key and suggested command
Runbooks state exact preconditions, stop conditions, evidence and rollback-only-current-action command
Backup runbooks distinguish online restore-drill-only backups from already-suspended eligible backups and provide no force, marker deletion or stale-sidecar recovery path
Work sessions bind SID hash/scope/generation/reason/start/end and never save prompt or secret content
Overlapping operator intervals count once and all failure/cancel/recovery/maintenance minutes enter the numerator
work_items.rollback: Revert only operator-action code, runbooks, tests and this task evidence.
stop_conditions: A manual state has no unique safe command or stop condition
Metrics require prompt, credential or free-text reason persistence
prohibited_actions: Do not send notifications
Do not store free-text cancel/operator reasons

work_items.title: Implement compat, cutover and evaluation command surfaces
work_items.goal: Expose read-only legacy compatibility verification, guarded per-repo cutover/rollback planning and fixed efficiency/reliability evaluation without performing production cutover.
preconditions: Operator action/runbook verification passes
Legacy ownership adapter, cutover policy and evaluation formulas are approved

in: compat verify and legacy evidence readers
Cutover/rollback guard evaluation and dry-run records
out: Running live cohort or production cutover

acceptance: Compat readers never write legacy DB/evidence and distinguish legacy from new-runtime proof
Cutover/rollback dry run checks zero active/closing work, ownership generation and rollback drill without changing owner
Efficiency evaluator uses fixed paired-case denominator/numerator rules and overlap normalization
Cohort evaluator enforces safety zeros, task composition, unattended/operator thresholds and no unresolved terminal state
Canary rollback requires still-valid prior profile, qualification and Authorization and never dynamically falls back
work_items.rollback: Revert only compat/evaluation command code, focused tests and this task evidence; no ownership or live state should change.
stop_conditions: A dry-run command can mutate repo ownership or live state
An evaluation metric permits excluding failed operator effort
prohibited_actions: Do not cut over a repository or install a scheduler
Do not run live pilots/cohorts

work_items.title: Complete Implementation Acceptance
work_items.goal: Run all fixed development gates, migrations, conformance, crash tests, backup/restore and runbook drills and create an append-only ImplementationAcceptanceRecord.
preconditions: All P1 implementation slices are complete
Baseline Approval remains active
acceptance: Every fixed offline gate passes
All crash and at-most-once invariants pass
Backup restore drill passes in a new root with same-SID key-envelope unwrap and tamper rejection
Disposable production-like tests prove post-activity staling, immutable restore intent replay and one eligible-to-restoring-to-consumed production restore without touching live state
Acceptance record binds code, baseline, toolchain and evidence hashes
P2 remains blocked
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime contracts verify
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime ruff check runtime/local-ai-runtime
work_items.rollback: Append an acceptance revocation record if later invalidated; never rewrite the acceptance record.
stop_conditions: Any gate, crash invariant or restore drill fails
Evidence cannot be independently verified
prohibited_actions: Do not run a live writer
Do not mark Full Q0 passed

work_items.title: Run Full Q0 and activate P2 Admission
work_items.goal: Qualify the real installed Codex/Git/Windows/sandbox/adapter/model/profile/keyring/network/filesystem stack and create a FullQ0Record before any live writer is allowed.
preconditions: Implementation Acceptance is active
Interactive operator is available for login and platform qualification
Production activation and rollback records verify
acceptance: Full Q0 passes with pinned toolchain/profile
Every required capability, deny probe and inventory hash is present
Real Windows probes prove case-clean environment construction, JOB_LIST plus exact HANDLE_LIST stdio inheritance, parent-end close/EOF and no ambient controller-sensitive handles
Real filesystem/DPAPI probes prove runtime-managed external evidence separation, purpose-bound key-envelope unwrap and suspended-only restore eligibility behavior
Full Q0 and P2 Admission activate as one generation
No writer, Git publication or task ref is created
A Q0 failure leaves P2 Admission false and produces one scoped OperatorAction
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime platform qualify --full --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime evidence verify --record FullQ0Record.v1 --json
work_items.rollback: Append a FullQ0 revocation, suspend platform claims and roll back activation; preserve all qualification evidence.
stop_conditions: Any Full Q0 probe fails or is ambiguous
Toolchain/profile/effective config drifts during qualification
Qualification would require a task writer
prohibited_actions: Do not run a pilot writer
Do not substitute repo-side simulation or legacy probes
Do not admit P2 on partial Q0

work_items.title: Run one low-risk self-host pilot
work_items.goal: After Full Q0/P2 Admission, activate B1 and execute exactly one approved low-risk commit-only self-host task under human initiation.
preconditions: Full Q0 and P2 Admission are active
Pilot repo and template qualification/Authorization are valid
Interactive operator explicitly starts the pilot
acceptance: Exactly one writer execution is committed
Task becomes commit-ready with all evidence verified
No unauthorized write, egress, duplicate writer or cleanup error occurs
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime evidence verify --task <task-id> --json
work_items.rollback: Suspend the platform, preserve evidence/ref, and roll back activation; never delete the task ref automatically.
stop_conditions: Full Q0/P2 Admission becomes inactive
Any security hard gate fires
Operator intervention is needed after writer start outside an approved recovery runbook
prohibited_actions: Do not schedule the pilot
Do not merge or push

work_items.title: Run five scheduled self-host observations
work_items.goal: Activate B2 for one promoted template and collect five scheduled commit-ready self-host observations with zero unresolved state.
preconditions: B1 pilot passed
Template canary and Authorization are current
acceptance: Five tasks are commit-ready or the phase fails
No unresolved recovery/reconcile/cleanup remains
Security hard gates remain zero
OperatorWorkSession data covers every manual minute
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime schedule status --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime eval baseline --cohort p3 --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime evidence verify --cohort p3 --json
work_items.rollback: Remove the scheduled task, suspend B2 and preserve all refs/evidence.
stop_conditions: Any security hard gate fires
A new failure class or unplanned operator intervention appears
prohibited_actions: Do not add a second writer
Do not expand to another repo

work_items.title: Qualify two target repositories and run the 30-task cohort
work_items.goal: Activate B3 only after two real repositories qualify, then measure safety, reliability, unattended success and operator efficiency over the fixed cohort.
preconditions: Five scheduled self-host observations pass
Two target repos and templates pass qualification/Authorization
acceptance: All security hard gates are zero
At least 25 tasks are commit-ready
Unattended success is at least 80 percent and manual intervention at most 20 percent
Mandatory gates/evidence/backup/recovery are 100 percent
Net operator minutes per success improves at least 50 percent
No unresolved state remains
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime eval compare --baseline <id> --cohort p4 --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime evidence verify --cohort p4 --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime backup restore-drill --cohort p4 --json
work_items.rollback: Suspend B3, keep qualified repos guarded, preserve refs/evidence and revert profile generation only when its prior Authorization remains valid.
stop_conditions: Any security hard gate fires
Any target repo leaves qualification
A new failure type or new manual intervention appears
prohibited_actions: Do not dynamically fall back model/profile
Do not waive safety gates

work_items.title: Cut over repositories one at a time and retire legacy writers
work_items.goal: Perform per-repo CAS cutover with rollback drills, then make legacy state read-only and remove legacy writers only after the 30-day zero-use window.
preconditions: P4 cohort passes
Per-repo zero-active and rollback guards pass
acceptance: Each repo changes owner by generation CAS
Rollback drill passes before each cutover
Legacy DB becomes read-only only after all target repos cut over and no active/closing work remains
Legacy writer removal occurs only after 30 days of zero legacy calls
Compatibility reader, historical evidence and task refs remain readable
verification: uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime repo ownership-status --all --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime repo rollback --drill --all --json
uv run --frozen --offline --project runtime/local-ai-runtime python -m local_ai_runtime compat verify --json
work_items.rollback: CAS each affected repo back to guarded_legacy while preserving all new-runtime state/evidence for audit.
stop_conditions: Any repo has active/nonterminal work
Rollback drill fails
A legacy call occurs during the retirement window
prohibited_actions: Do not bulk cut over repositories
Do not delete legacy evidence or task refs
